etymology.description: semantic 目(目) (mù, 'eye') + phonetic 艮(艮) (gěn) -> eye
etymology.interpretation: The character 眼(yǎn, 'eye') combines 目(目) (mù, 'eye'), representing its meaning, with 艮(艮) (gěn), which provides the pronunciation. This construction directly associates the character with the concept of an eye.

examples.chinese: 她的眼睛很大(她的眼睛很大)。
我右眼有点疼(我右眼有點疼)。
examples.english: My right eye hurts a bit.

examples.chinese: 他的眼神总是很温柔(他的眼神總是很溫柔)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnshén zǒng shì hěn wēnróu.
